behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: This failure boundary is at real Git rename detection plus CLI parsing/output wiring. Mocked Git responses can hide mismatches in rename status formatting or flags, so a real repo + real git invocation is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against real git state, and assert report shows 1 rename, 0 add for that file, and 0 delete for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff behavior depends on real git diff output and subprocess handling. Mocks often miss edge formatting and byte-related behavior, so they can falsely pass.
behaviors.test_description: Create and commit a binary file, change it, run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary change is included in counts/category output.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure occurs at real git command behavior in an unborn-HEAD repository. Mocking can hide actual exit codes/stderr semantics from git.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero exit (or documented non-error behavior) and an empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against real repository history and git query behavior, not just local counting logic. Real subprocess + repo state are part of the failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run the CLI for the target range, and compare reported counts to expected counts derived from git history ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics are defined by real git index/working-tree behavior. Mocked repositories or fake command outputs can miss real index edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent file changes once as staged and once as unstaged (in controlled runs), execute CLI in both states, and assert normalized report outputs are identical.
